local function checkDateTable (t)
  _G.D = os.date("*t", t)
  assert(os.time(D) == t)
  load(os.date([[assert(D.year==%Y and D.month==%m and D.day==%d and
    D.hour==%H and D.min==%M and D.sec==%S and
    D.wday==%w+1 and D.yday==%j)]], t))()
  _G.D = nil
end

checkDateTable(os.time())
if not _port then
  -- assume that time_t can represent these values
  checkDateTable(0)
  checkDateTable(1)
  checkDateTable(1000)
  checkDateTable(0x7fffffff)

content.content:
print("testing date/time")

assert(os.date("") == "")
assert(os.date("!") == "")
assert(os.date("\0\0") == "\0\0")
assert(os.date("!\0\0") == "\0\0")
local x = string.rep("a", 10000)
assert(os.date(x) == x)
local t = os.time()
D = os.date("*t", t)
assert(os.date(string.rep("%d", 1000), t) ==
       string.rep(os.date("%d", t), 1000))
assert(os.date(string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))

local function checkDateTable (t)
  _G.D = os.date("*t", t)
  assert(os.time(D) == t)
  load(os.date([[assert(D.year==%Y and D.month==%m and D.day==%d and
    D.hour==%H and D.min==%M and D.sec==%S and
    D.wday==%w+1 and D.yday==%j)]], t))()
  _G.D = nil
end

checkDateTable(os.time())
if not _port then

content.content:
5:# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
44:PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

# run_official_test.sh <test_file>

# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then

    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"

    src="$(cat "$src_file")"

        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then

        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true

delta.text: Found the root cause. The failing assertion is at combined-line 802 = `assert(os.date(
string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))`. Real `strftime("%%", ...)` em
its the literal `%`, but our Phase-A `os_date` stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_l

ib.rs:520` silently drops every specifier, so the result is `""` instead of 100 `%`s.

Let me also look at the broader test flow to understand if other n
earby tests rely on `%%` too.

content.text:
Found the root cause. The failing assertion is at combined-line 802 = `assert(os.date(string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))`. Real `strftime("%%", ...)` emits the literal `%`, but our Phase-A `os_date` stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs:520` silently drops every specifier, so the result is `""` instead of 100 `%`s.

Let me also look at the broader test flow to understand if other nearby tests rely on `%%` too.

delta.text: Now I'll add
the `%%` → `%` case (a real fix — str
ftime defines `%%` to emit a literal `%` regardless of any TmFields content; all other specifiers st
ay TODO).

content.text: Now I'll add the `%%` → `%` case (a real fix — strftime defines `%%` to emit a literal `%` regardless of any TmFields content; all other specifiers stay TODO).

ertion `assert(os.date(string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))` (comb
ined-line 802) needs `%%` to expand to a literal `%`; with the stub it

ix:** Added a real, data-independent branch in `os_date` for the `%%` specifier — when `oplen == 1

mFields not yet derived from `t`) remain TODO(

content.text:
Build is green (warnings only, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added that needs removal.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs:520` — the `os_date` strftime loop matches a conversion specifier via `check_strftime_option` but then emits nothing for every specifier (Phase-A stub). The failing assertion `assert(os.date(string.rep("%", 200)) == string.rep("%", 100))` (combined-line 802) needs `%%` to expand to a literal `%`; with the stub it expanded to nothing, so `""` ≠ `"%%..."` and the assertion fired.

**Fix:** Added a real, data-independent branch in `os_date` for the `%%` specifier — when `oplen == 1 && cc[1] == b'%'`, push one `b'%'` into the result buffer. Other specifiers (which depend on TmFields not yet derived from `t`) remain TODO(port). This is the actual strftime semantics for `%%`, not a workaround.

**New state:** I did not re-run the test (per the instructions — the mega-loop re-tests). Build passes (`cargo build -p lua-cli` clean of errors). The previously-failing assertion at combined-line 802 should now pass; the next likely failure shifts forward to combined-line ~807 (`load(os.date([[assert(D.year==%Y ...)]], t))()`), which requires real `%Y/%m/%d/...` expansion and is a separate Phase-B work item.
